title: ulmus campestris subsp. amplifolia
intro: Ulmus campestris subsp. amplifolia, commonly known as Asian Elm, is a plant species within the Ulmaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties and is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The health benefits of Asian Elm include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its therapeutic actions such as anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antiseptic effects. The bioactive constituents present in Asian Elm include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
benefits: Ulmus campestris subsp. amplifolia has many benefits, such as being a natural remedy for various health issues, including reducing inflammation and pain, treating respiratory problems like bronchitis and asthma, and even helping to manage diabetes and high blood pressure. Its extracts have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it effective against fungal and bacterial infections, while also providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can improve skin health and reduce the risk of certain cancers. Additionally, the plant's leaves and bark are used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, and its wood is valued for its durability and resistance to pests and decay, making it a sustainable option for construction and furniture-making. Overall, the benefits of Ulmus campestris subsp. amplifolia contribute to improved health, reduced risk of disease, and a more sustainable and environmentally-friendly lifestyle.
constituents: Ulmus campestris subsp. amplifolia has many active constituents, such as flavonoids (quercetin and kaempferol), phenolic acids (gallic acid and ellagic acid), and tannins (gallic tannins and ellagic tannins), which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. These compounds have been found to improve people's lives by reducing oxidative stress, inflammation, and bacterial infections, ultimately contributing to improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of chronic diseases, and enhanced overall well-being. Additionally, the plant's constituents, including sterols (beta-sitosterol and stigmasterol), saponins, and alkaloids, have been reported to exhibit anti-cancer, anti-diabetic, and anti-hypertensive effects, making it a valuable resource in the development of novel medicinal therapies. By harnessing the medicinal properties of Ulmus campestris subsp. amplifolia, researchers and healthcare professionals aim to create more effective treatments for a range of health conditions, ultimately improving the quality of life for individuals worldwide.
parts: Ulmus campestris subsp. amplifolia has many medicinal parts, such as leaves, flowers, and roots, which are used to treat various health issues. The leaves of the Ulmus campestris subsp. amplifolia plant contain flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots of the plant contain phenolic compounds, including salicin, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. The flowers of the plant also contain flavonoids, such as rutin and isorhapontin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects: Ulmus campestris subsp. amplifolia can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and skin irritation. Consuming large quantities of the plant's leaves or bark may lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to the presence of toxic compounds. In severe cases, improper use of ulmus campestris subsp. amplifolia can cause anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention. Additionally, long-term exposure to the plant's extracts may also lead to liver damage and other organ-related problems.
